Goffs Prix Robert Papin (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Hasn't looked back since debut second and has progressed with every run, landing the Group 3 Prix du Bois over 6f last time on what is likely to be similar ground; won twice here prior to that so obvious chance up against the two stablemates.

RaceName: Goffs Prix Robert Papin (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Made good progress on every start, and comes into this having won the Windsor Castle Stakes (6f, good to firm) at Royal Ascot about a month ago; shaped well over 7f prior to that behind another subsequent royal meeting winner, so unlikely to want this run at a dwadle.

RaceName: Goffs Prix Robert Papin (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Progressed, like her two rivals, with every run and was last seen finishing third in a 6f Group 2 at the Curragh for fillies on good to firm; figures suggest she has a bit to find but is the only one that gets the sex allowance.

RaceName: Prix de la Pepiniere (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
Prix de la Pepiniere (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
Prix de la Pepiniere (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
Prix de la Pepiniere (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
Prix de la Pepiniere (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
Prix de la Pepiniere (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Took a huge step forward when getting to within a head of Carry The Flag in his second career start dropped back to 5f at Naas in May (good, 80-1); ran keenly when 6l behind Carry The Flag in a Naas Listed race earlier this month; down in grade now and claims on the basis of his penultimate run; holds a Group 1 entry here and sets the standard on 93.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: 10,000euros foal, 23,000euros yearling; bought for 300,000euros at a Breeze-Up in May; ninth foal; half-brother to four winners inc Ruby's Profit (5f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 97), Miss Power (5f; 95) and Between Hills (6f 2yo; 94); dam Listed placed over 5f as 2yo but did not win (92?); holds Group 1 entries here; yard does well with juveniles, but is a little cold at the moment.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Well-bred No Nay Never colt was a 6.5l fifth on debut at Cork when stable second string (6f, good, 6-1); dropped back to 5f at Bellewstown and improved on his initial effort when a 2.5l second of eight (good); obvious chance with Moore up for the first time, but by no means unbeatable at the same time.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal; dam 5f-1m maiden (RPR 65), sister to Listed-placed 5f/6f winner Gold Filigree, out of 5f 2yo winning half-sister to 1m Group 2 winner Emerald Commander; not super appealing on paper.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: One of two for trainer; has started slowly in both runs to date; improved on his moderate debut over 6f here in June when a 1.25l fourth of eight at Fairyhouse in first-time blinkers (retained) earlier this month (6f, good); drops to 5f, but may get involved if getting away quicker.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: 160,000gns yearling finished second of six behind an Aidan O'Brien-trained odds-on chance at Dundalk on debut (5f); shaped like he will have learned plenty from that and a tongue tie and cheekpieces have been added; in the mix.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Hung right when sixth over 5f on debut at Cork (soft); well beaten when upped to 6.5f at Listowel next time; a bit better back over 5f at Bellewstown last time; hood goes on, but a lot of improvement needed.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Minzaal filly cost 160,000euros as a yearling; well beaten on debut when sixth of seven and 3.5l behind Southern Boulevard; others more likely.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: One of two for trainer; No Nay Never filly cost 110,000euros as a yearling; relatively unfancied for her Naas debut (5f, good) and was beaten 9l; improvement probable but Keane rides La Tache.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: 42,000euros yearling; (30,000gns vendor 2yo); dam unplaced 5f/6f (RPR 58), sister to 5f winner Heiba, half-sister to winners Golden Stunner (useful 7f/1m) and Colonel Carter (1m-1m2f), out of half-sister to dam of top miler Canford Cliffs; best watched for the moment.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Partners Of Irish Sport Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Has beaten just one rival in two maiden runs over 5f/6.5f on different ground; tongue tie on but of no appeal.

RaceName: Sharon Pardy 60th Birthday Celebration Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won three consecutive times (once over C&D) in May/June and held good lead before falling at third-last flight in recent 2m2f handicap here; leading contender back in novice company.

RaceName: Sharon Pardy 60th Birthday Celebration Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Not disgraced in the second of his two bumpers last season but tailed off when switched to hurdling this month; tongue-tie now added.

RaceName: Sharon Pardy 60th Birthday Celebration Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Awr Consultancy Ltd & The Folly Partners
Spotlight: Returned from break in career-best form over fences this spring and subsequently exploited her novice hurdle status at Hexham (2m, good), albeit unimpressive in justifying skinny odds; suited by fast ground and features prominently in calculations.

RaceName: Sharon Pardy 60th Birthday Celebration Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten here on both recent hurdling starts (2m2f/2m), the latter after a wind op; can't be recommended.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Trailed home last of ten in 7f AW maiden at Newcastle having blown the start; needs to break much better and take a huge step forward.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mrs T Burns And Rjh Ltd
Spotlight: £16,000 yearling; Gregorian half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Magistery (RPR 67), out of 7f scorer (including 2yo; 95); suitably bred; market support would heighten interest.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Needs big improvement for a physical tweak, namely the gelding operation he's undergone since posting two duck eggs in May.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Met traffic en route to a promising fifth of 15, best of the newcomers, in the long-established Rous Selling Stakes at York (6f, good); shaped as if 7f will suit; the form pick.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Holds weak form claims, having finished last in both starts, latest in seven-runner maiden over C&D.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mrs D Dalby And J Knotts
Spotlight: 6,000gns yearling; by Sergei Prokofiev and first foal of 1m2f AW scorer (RPR 62); stable had a winning 2yo newcomer (SP 28-1) at Redcar last month; one to bear in mind.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Needed the outing/experience on debut; fared better, despite a tardy start, at Epsom (7f, good) where he stayed on for third of eight; respected with another step forward plausible.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Belardo half-brother to winners Daydreama (6f 2yo, for Tim Easterby; RPR 92) and Tuddenham Green (2m hurdle); dam 2m AW scorer (58); check the market signals.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Finished last of ten in 7f event at Ayr; needs to leave that form well behind and is a similar type to Bedwyr.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Always behind in 7f maiden at Carlisle, beating just one rival in a field of 11; seems one for handicaps later on.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner
Spotlight: From a family that her trainer knows well; however, her form suggests nurseries will be much more suitable shortly.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Showed some promise with fifth of 11 in Chester seller (7f, good to soft); posted a lesser effort on AW later in June but may be capable of improvement returned to turf.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Not particularly solid on her Doncaster efforts (7f) having finished eighth of 13 then last of seven; however, this is probably an easier assignment; may raise her game.

RaceName: Racing TV Free Trial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 4,000gns yearling; by Cracksman out of once-raced sister to winners Pelerin (7f/1m Listed) and Base Note (smart 9.7f-1m4f); yard is only 1-28 with 2yos this term.

RaceName: Cavalor Equine Nutrition Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Well-bred filly is rated 76 after four starts; went close at Thurles on her second and final start last term (1m, good to yielding); hasn't really progressed in two runs this year, the latest in cheekpieces at Naas (10.5f, good); Moore rides White Sand Beach.

RaceName: Cavalor Equine Nutrition Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Calyx filly made a positive impression when running green but taking the runner-up spot ahead of subsequent winner Vauntingly at Gowran (1m2f, good); only an 8l third at Fairyhouse next time (1m2f, good), but was 2l behind the runner-up Mai Dahlia, who won easily next time; interesting.

RaceName: Cavalor Equine Nutrition Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to French Listed-placed 6f-7f winner Summer Sail (RPR 103); dam unraced half-sister to 6f-1m winner Beau Jardine, out of Listed-placed 7f/1m winner; Browne McMonagle rides Star Performer.

RaceName: Cavalor Equine Nutrition Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: One of two for trainer; Sea The Stars filly; 75,000yearling, 160,000euros 2yo; second foal; dam 5f/6f winner (inc 2yo/AW/German Group 3; RPR 101), half-sister to winners Back To Brussels (Listed-placed 5f 2yo/6f) and Green Sense (6f 2yo Group 2), is granddaughter of US 6f Listed winner; appeals on paper and chosen by Browne McMonagle.

RaceName: Cavalor Equine Nutrition Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Down the field on her only juvenile start, but much better on her return at Rocommon last month when fourth of 12 (7.5f, good); ran green that day so could improve again, but will need to.

RaceName: Cavalor Equine Nutrition Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Slowly away when beaten 17l on debut over 1m at Naas; likely needs more time.

RaceName: Cavalor Equine Nutrition Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Placed six times in maidens from 7f-11.5f on different ground; longer trip didn't bring improvement from her at Killarney last time when again runner-up (11.5f, good); she's expensive to follow, but  sets the standard and her day is sure to come someday soon.

RaceName: George & Dragon Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won over hurdles last season and made pretty good start to chasing career when third of seven at Bangor (2m4f, good; wore cheekpieces) in April; weakened out of contention on run-in at Worcester (2m7f, good; first-time visor) this month but drops back in trip here and remains of interest.

RaceName: George & Dragon Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 1-9 over hurdles; makes chasing debut from a workable mark but has become very inconsistent and was well beaten in first-time cheekpieces last month (2m7f); drops back in trip with visor tried here; not easy to predict.

RaceName: George & Dragon Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 0-4 over fences for new stable this year but ran well over 2m1f here on last two appearances, losing out only by half a length on latter occasion; should be fine over this longer trip; good chance.

RaceName: George & Dragon Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Both wins over hurdles when making all, one here on fast ground; ridden with more restraint since returning this year but was in contention before last-fence fall at Bangor (2m4f, good) two starts ago, and last month's Uttoxeter third was respectable; can go well again and it will be interesting to see whether she reverts to more positive tactics.

RaceName: George & Dragon Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well suited by this track when a useful hurdler for Nicky Henderson a few years ago but, following a long absence, has been safely held on both starts for new stable this season, latterly back here in a three-runner race; needs a revival on first chasing attempt since 2022.

RaceName: Luke Jobson Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Son of Persian King who has shaped well to be placed on both his runs, a clear second of nine in 1m novice at Newcastle in February; off since but he remains with potential and merits serious consideration.

RaceName: Luke Jobson Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 1,000euros yearling; Galileo Gold gelding; half-brother to winners Philos (1m4f/1m6f; RPR 67) and Cham's Pride (Spanish 9.5f); dam, French 9.5f turf/10.7f AW winner, is a sister to 1m Listed winner Toungi; hooded for his debut and the market can guide.

RaceName: Luke Jobson Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 18-1, well-held fifth of six in maiden at Beverley (8.4f, good) on his debut last month; this son of Mondialiste needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Luke Jobson Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Capable of fair form but not proving the easiest to catch right; one of his better efforts when third of nine in 7f Catterick handicap 11 days ago; however, only third of four when favourite for 6f maiden at Ripon on Saturday.

RaceName: Luke Jobson Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 5,000gns yearling; closely related to 5f AW winner Traditionelle (RPR 73) and a half-brother to seven other winners, notably Universal Gleam (1m-1m2f; RPR 99) and Fools And Kings (9.4f AW/1m4f turf; RPR 89); dam 5f winner (RPR 80); the betting can prove an accurate indicator for this Mattmu gelding on his first run.

RaceName: Luke Jobson Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well held in 7f Musselburgh maiden in November and 7f Southwell novice on her return two weeks ago; significantly more is required.

RaceName: John Bircham And Stephen "Jagger" Hayes Memorial Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: AW winner over 8.6f for Ed Bethell, his only success in eight starts; didn't exactly sign off for that yard in great form and was sold on for 1,500gns.

RaceName: John Bircham And Stephen "Jagger" Hayes Memorial Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 0-6 for William Knight, though latest fourth at Lingfield (extended 1m3f) was one of his better efforts; new trainer, whom he joined for 10,500gns, goes with a hood for hurdling debut.

RaceName: John Bircham And Stephen "Jagger" Hayes Memorial Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: One competitive run in six Flat starts, though finished tailed off in his only run this year (1m2f) after being gelded; goes hurdling in a weak race but with enough to prove.

RaceName: John Bircham And Stephen "Jagger" Hayes Memorial Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dam unraced sister to useful bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Tikkanbar; best watch on debut.

RaceName: John Bircham And Stephen "Jagger" Hayes Memorial Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten a little under 8l on debut at Stratford where she got pestered up front and weakened; the second has won again since and this looks weaker.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Supporting Irish Sport Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Blue Point filly; third foal; dam French 1m2f winner, closely related to Australian 6.5f Group 2 winner Home Of The Brave.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Supporting Irish Sport Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Satisfactory debut at Leopardstown, but has 4 1/2l to find with Ibelieveicanfly who has picked up further experience into the bargain.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Supporting Irish Sport Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Half-sister by No Nay Never to three winners, including Concert Hall, a Group 3-winning filly for this stable; dam, by Galileo, won the Oaks, sister to Group 3 winners at 1m2f and 1m6f; a leading apprentice aboard, but looks like the yard's third string.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Supporting Irish Sport Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Mehmas filly; E135,000 yearling; dam 7f 2yo winner; finished 1 1/4l in front of her shorter-priced stablemate Shi Yang when third of seven in a 5f maiden at Navan early this month.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Supporting Irish Sport Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: E120,000 Y; seventh foal; Lope De Vega filly is closely related to 5f AW winner Real Encounter, half-sister to four winners, including Real Appeal (eight wins, including a 1m Group 2); champion jockey prefers the once-raced Citrus Springs.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Supporting Irish Sport Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Flaxman S
Spotlight: Well clear of fifth-placed Citrus Springs when ridden by Ryan Moore on her debut at Leopardstown; had Wayne Lordan aboard when chasing home Moore's mount Alpha over C&D three weeks ago; form was given a strong boost by a win for Livenka at Fairyhouse last week.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Supporting Irish Sport Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 6-4 favourite on debut over 5f at Naas last month, weakened late into fifth; failed to improve as much as might have been expected when fourth over the same course and trip on second start, 1 1/4 behind her bigger-priced stablemate Fly Smart.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Supporting Irish Sport Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Half-sister by No Nay Never to Listed winner Boogie Woogie; dam won the Irish and Yorkshire Oaks; half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 1 winner Crusade; Ryan Moore on Ibelieveicanfly.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Supporting Irish Sport Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 10,500gns yearling; eleventh foal; half-sister by State Of Rest to five winners, best of them a 7f Listed scorer.

RaceName: Dave Shrader Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual chase winner (2m5f/2m3f) this spring, after a break; didn't see out 3m on latest outing but drops back in trip for this switch back to hurdling and his current mark is workable; respected.

RaceName: Dave Shrader Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Fair Flat-racer who returned to hurdling with two fast-ground course wins (2m/2m2f) this season; drew nicely clear on run-in on latter occasion and commands respect in current mood.

RaceName: Dave Shrader Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Held up in rear and showed nothing more than glimmers of promise in three 2m maiden/novice hurdles this spring but has a tongue-tie fitted and moves up in trip for this handicap debut; improvement possible.

RaceName: Dave Shrader Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-25 over hurdles but has run some good races at this track, most recently when close second over 2m (good to firm) three weeks ago; also suited by this longer trip and can make her presence felt again.

RaceName: Dave Shrader Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Safely held when fifth in first-time cheekpieces here (2m, good) last month and now 0-10 over hurdles; upped in trip and tried in a visor today; improvement required.

RaceName: Dave Shrader Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Unplaced all eight hurdling starts for David Jeffreys and absent since pulled up over 3m in November; 3lb out of the weights for this stable debut and could be considered only if lively in the betting.

RaceName: Dave Shrader Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Unexposed over hurdles after only five runs but was beaten about 23l when seventh of 12 here (2m, good) on last month's handicap debut and is effectively 4lb higher today.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-French filly who capitalised on a reduced mark when gaining a first win at the 14th attempt in 1m6f handicap here last month; back up 4lb but another bold showing is on the cards with this return to 2m a likely plus.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: A four-time scorer (including here in 2025); in good nick without winning this season, finding drop to 1m4f against him when a staying-on sixth of 12 at Newcastle last time; this veteran needs considering back up in trip.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Winless since a breakthrough victory at Wolverhampton (2m) in October but she got back on song with third of five in 2m Catterick handicap in May; not ruled out.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Is 0-22 and she came in last of six in 1m4f handicap at Beverley 26 days ago; has her stamina to prove here too.

RaceName: Happy 80th Birthday Ted Hull Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Essentially disappointing since finishing second in her first bumper at Newbury (soft) and was beaten miles behind a stablemate on hurdle debut at Plumpton (2m4f, good).

RaceName: Happy 80th Birthday Ted Hull Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 1m4f/1m6f wins for Charlie Johnston; now 0-11 over hurdles and was disappointing in a low-grade handicap here only recently; however, there is placed form in her record and she could have some part to play.

RaceName: Happy 80th Birthday Ted Hull Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Promise in bumpers and again when runner-up to a useful odds-on winner in a Worcester maiden; should be on the scene.

RaceName: Happy 80th Birthday Ted Hull Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won a Southwell bumper on good ground in May but beaten a fair way since, including on hurdle debut here; still rates a likely contender if tapping back into the promise of her win.

RaceName: Happy 80th Birthday Ted Hull Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won her third bumper start (good ground) but was beaten 16l into fourth on her hurdle debut and since then has finished out the back in a 1m6f maiden at Goodwood; has ability though and this isn't a good maiden.

RaceName: Happy 80th Birthday Ted Hull Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Not a lot to show for two bumper runs and was 125-1 when tailed off on hurdle debut here.

RaceName: Happy 80th Birthday Ted Hull Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Low-level bumper form even when she was placed; well behind Getmyfriend here 16 days ago.

RaceName: Happy 80th Birthday Ted Hull Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Posh Pundit Racing Club & G Nicholls
Spotlight: Fair Flat racer at up to 1m6f with 4-22 strike-rate; close second on hurdle debut at Stratford (good to firm) before finishing a well-held third behind a classy sort back there a month ago; can go well.

RaceName: Glenroyal Hotel And Shoda Cafe Minstrel Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Group 1 Lockinge winner in 2024 and Group 2 winner at Goodwood the same year; on a losing run since but his runner-up finish in a Leopardstown Group 3 showed definite signs of encouragement (7f, soft) when ridden by Keane for the first time; his wins came on good so if he repeats that latest run he is firmly in the mix.

RaceName: Glenroyal Hotel And Shoda Cafe Minstrel Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Entering legendary course specialist status after adding a tenth win at the venue in a Listed race last time (6f, good); prior to that he won a C&D Listed contest in March (soft to heavy) when seeing off East Hampton by 1.5l; got closest to subsequent Group 1 winner Comanche Brave when a 2l second in a 6f Group 2 here on penultimate (good); always a danger around here and should go well again.

RaceName: Glenroyal Hotel And Shoda Cafe Minstrel Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Won his maiden over 6f here in 2024 (yielding to soft) and bagged the Madrid handicap at Naas last season (7f, soft to heavy); best run since was when beaten a neck by Diego Velazquez in this race last year (good to yielding); beaten 1.5l behind Big Gossey in a C&D Listed race on return (soft to heavy); not at his best at Leopardstown last time; appears to give his best on softer ground.

RaceName: Glenroyal Hotel And Shoda Cafe Minstrel Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Okay fourth in the Group 3 Ballycorus Stakes at Leopardstown on return (7f, soft) and built on that when making all and keeping on well to win a 1m Listed race here last time (good); cheekpieces added and drop back to 7f should be okay; having just his sixth career run so improvement still feasible.

RaceName: Glenroyal Hotel And Shoda Cafe Minstrel Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Gained first win at stakes level when taking a Listed fillies' event at Navan last month (1m, good); found wanting in a deeper race over 1m here on Irish Derby weekend next time; resumed winning ways when landing a Group 3 at Leopardstown last time (7f, good), however, the proximity of an 83-rated rival (1l) to her takes the sheen off the form a bit.

RaceName: Glenroyal Hotel And Shoda Cafe Minstrel Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Won three times as a juvenile, including the valuable Goffs Million over course and distance; finished 2.5l behind Power Blue on seasonal return at Leopardstown when seventh of 11 (7f, good to yielding); faced a tough task in the French 2,000 Guineas; weakened into a modest ninth in the Jersey Stakes last time; others preferred.

RaceName: Glenroyal Hotel And Shoda Cafe Minstrel Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Won a Group 1 over 6f here on his final juvenile start (good); runner-up behind subsequent Royal Ascot and Group 1 winner on his reappearance in a Group 3 over 7f at Leopardstown in April (good to yielding); probably not up to the top level as shown in three Group 1 runs over 1m since, but his latest two were meritable; big danger dropping in class and trip; top rated of the field too; stable a little cold currently though.

RaceName: Patrick's 60th Celebration Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Flopped badly when favourite at Hexham last month and needs to put that behind him but looked in good nick when making all for clearcut success at Hereford (3m1f, good) two runs ago; dangerous if back at that level.

RaceName: Patrick's 60th Celebration Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Improving 6yo who kept on resolutely to score over C&D (good to firm) 19 days ago and has now won three times since the spring; another 5lb higher now but a likely contender again.

RaceName: Patrick's 60th Celebration Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Not at best on the Flat this year (albeit still capable of useful form) and managed only fourth of six when warm favourite for latest hurdle race, at Worcester (2m, good) in May; that said, he's a stout stayer on the Flat and could have untapped potential over this new trip over jumps.

RaceName: Patrick's 60th Celebration Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Regressive 10yo who has been lightly raced in recent years and returned from a break with two low-key runs this spring; pulled up (lost action) in first-time cheekpieces (retained) recently; revival needed.

RaceName: Patrick's 60th Celebration Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Slightly disappointing at Perth last week but was placed on consecutive outings beforehand, once over C&D on fast ground; swaps blinkers for refitted visor (worn once on Flat in 2020); no surprise if she's on the premises.

RaceName: Darley Prix Chloe (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Half-sister to Saleymm, Saddadd and Almaqam, so bred to be a Group performer; made it fourth time lucky when winning at Lyon Parilly last time over a mile with a cosy success; step up in trip may bring about more improvement.

RaceName: Darley Prix Chloe (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Making steady improvement and beat Eklleel a short-neck in March over a mile on very soft; shaped nicely in Listed events since without winning one, albeit behind Maintain last time, and should be in the mix for a place.

RaceName: Darley Prix Chloe (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Useful for Charlie Appleby before joining this stable, and has two standout pieces of form, one of them last time in a mile Group 2 here over a furlong shorter, when beaten just under 3l; claims if producing her best form up a furlong in distance.

RaceName: Darley Prix Chloe (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Half-sister to the classy New Ground; won on debut and produced some respectable efforts this season, including last time under this rider in a 1m2f Listed event at Longchamp, when Ecija was third; may still be capable of better.

RaceName: Darley Prix Chloe (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Mostly exposed filly, who has run creditably at a decent level throughout her career; shown some improvement for this stable in 2026 but needs to do better than last time as Naas to pose a threat; stays beyond 1m2f and handles a sound surface.

RaceName: Darley Prix Chloe (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Nowhere near her best in the Premio Oaks d'Italia (1m2.5f) when last seen despite having the run of the race, so better judged on her Listed win at Newmarket before that over 1m2f on good to firm from the front; could be hard to catch if allowed to dominate.

RaceName: Darley Prix Chloe (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Only win came on the AW but mostly on the up during her career and capable of a good effort, but was beaten 9l when last seen in May on very soft in a C&D Listed event; can be involved if the fitting of blinkers off a break sharpens her up.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Signed off for 2025 at Newmarket (7f) in November and in good nick this season until coming in last of six over C&D in May; the sort to bounce back after a break.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Headstrong sort but he's taken his form up a notch since cheekpieces went on, scoring at Pontefract (1m) in June before beaten a nose when a clear second at Doncaster (1m) since having hung badly left in front; not the easiest of rides but a player once more here.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: A two-time 1m scorer at Newcastle last autumn; yet to hit top form this season though, only seventh of 14 back over 1m there last month; mark is slipping but he still needs to take a step forward.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Ended a long losing run at Newcastle (1m) in June but only 17th in 7f York handicap since; he needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: It's now 13 starts since his last success but he comes here in good form, challenging wide when fourth of nine in 1m handicap at Pontefract 12 days ago; weighted to go well eased 1lb.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Ended 2025 out of sorts after 1m2f Wetherby success last June; off 11 months before coming in a well-held eighth of 11 in 1m1f Carlisle handicap on his return last month; this course scorer needs to take a major step forward.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Resumed winning ways over C&D in June and backed it up with a solid third of eight at Doncaster (1m) 16 days ago; ought to be thereabouts again.

RaceName: Dick Oliver At Newton Abbot Races Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 0-9 as a hurdler but only his fourth run over fences when making light work of The Gypsy Davey over C&D (add 23yds) six days ago; penalised accordingly but Paddy Hanlon's claim takes care of 5lb of that.

RaceName: Dick Oliver At Newton Abbot Races Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual chase winner from 20 attempts over fences; returned to form when second to It's A Breeze here last week where he wasn't unlucky despite some late interference; should run well with a repeat, but can be inconsistent.

RaceName: Dick Oliver At Newton Abbot Races Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 10yo who has come a long way down the weights and he's had a sticky period since finishing second at Market Rasen five runs back; however, coming back from 3m could be a good move.

RaceName: Dick Oliver At Newton Abbot Races Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Had plenty of racing under both codes and has been regressing for a while now; chase debut.

RaceName: Dick Oliver At Newton Abbot Races Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Limited hurdler who was pulled up on chase debut in January; subsequent pointing exploits aren't overly compelling.

RaceName: Dick Oliver At Newton Abbot Races Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 1-12 as a chaser; good and bad in his last six races over hurdles, finishing tailed off in the last two; risky.

RaceName: Dick Oliver At Newton Abbot Races Handicap Chase
Spotlight: No wins in 14; ran better in his third chase over C&D in May, beaten about 13l behind one having a good summer; 2lb out of the weights.

RaceName: Dick Oliver At Newton Abbot Races Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Record of 4-8 in points but 16 defeats under rules include eight chases; ran no race last time and she's 2lb wrong.

RaceName: Racing World 50th Anniversary Irish EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice as a juvenile, including over 6f at this venue; won a 7f Fairyhouse handicap last year; well below that level on final two starts of the campaign; looked an awkward ride on her reappearance in a 7f premier handicap here last month; may simply have been ring-rusty; should improve from the run, and first-time cheekpieces may help.

RaceName: Racing World 50th Anniversary Irish EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins last year, including this event; career best when landing a highly competitive fillies' handicap at Leopardstown in September; has run five times this season, only once showing her best form; two lesser displays since a creditable third placing at Gowran in April; 10lb higher than 12 months ago.

RaceName: Racing World 50th Anniversary Irish EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins last season, including handicaps at Leopardstown (1m, good to firm) and Tipperary (7.5f, good); out of her depth in a Group 3 on seasonal debut; below form on soft at Leopardstown; much more like it on quick going over 7f here last time, chasing home one of the stars of the season, Meriden, who has won a Listed race to complete a five-timer; strong chance.

RaceName: Racing World 50th Anniversary Irish EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent in maidens and handicaps before breakthrough win in a 1m maiden at Gowran on her tenth start; got away with a 2lb rise but her chance was dismissed by punters when she ran in a 1m1f premier handicap here three weeks ago; 33-1 then, not disgraced in mid-division after meeting trouble in running; visor replaces cheekpieces worn then.

RaceName: Racing World 50th Anniversary Irish EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to make an impact in three maidens at 7f/1m; no significant improvement when upped to 1m4f for her handicap debut at Limerick; a different story when tackling an extended 1m on better ground at Fairyhouse, travelled well and took command under two furlongs down to score easily; has turned a corner and can remain competitive despite a 12lb rise.

RaceName: Racing World 50th Anniversary Irish EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Rarely runs a bad race; won a maiden at Dundalk in March; not beaten far in a 7f handicap at this venue in May; second in a small-field conditions race at Leopardstown, before third behind the same winner in a Killarney handicap on Monday; first-time cheekpieces; 2lb wrong, but good 5lb claimer.

RaceName: Silver Fox Classic Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Free-going front-runner who won five 2m chases in early part of last season; comfortably reeled in on both outings since returning from a break this spring, though, and comes here with a point to prove.

RaceName: Silver Fox Classic Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 3-10 over fences, the latest win gained at Newton Abbot (2m, good to soft) last autumn; returned from break with respectable fourth (a nose behind Sir Tivo) in race fought out by two progressive rivals at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last month and has a big role to play if that outing brought him on.

RaceName: Silver Fox Classic Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Progressive 6yo with 3-4 strike-rate over fences, including two fast-ground C&D wins this season; the form of last month's 20l success in a three-runner race is tricky to evaluate (odds-on favourite below par) but a 4lb rise could have been worse and he may still be on the upgrade.

RaceName: Silver Fox Classic Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Back in prime form when holding on well to score over C&D (good) on last month's chase return and now 4-10 over fences; recent 3lb rise does not look excessive and there's a good chance he'll be right in the thick of things again.

RaceName: Silver Fox Classic Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Veteran with 11 chase wins on his CV, including 2023 edition of this race; returns here after respectable third behind two progressive rivals at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last month but Centara is unlikely to allow him to play his preferred front-running role.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Successful in Britain and Bahrain during his spell with George Scott; gave impression something was amiss when last seen (had wind surgery since); current mark, combined with carrying a big weight, makes this his stiffest handicap assignment.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Comfortably prevailed in Doncaster handicap (1m2f, good to firm) on seasonal debut, following up his autumn AW success (1m, AW) and taking record to 2-3; the only unexposed contender in this field and commands respect with further improvement on the cards.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Scored three times (5f/7f) last term for previous yard; inconsistent over 1m this season and failed to beat a rival last time but best form gives him a fighting chance off current mark; continues to steadily rise in distance and now has a crack at 1m2f.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor
Spotlight: Scored three times, including over C&D, in 2024; has built up a long losing sequence since but is an in-form contender, half-length second when favourite at Carlisle most recently; on an attractive mark and Jake Dickson takes off a bonus 7lb; possibilities.

RaceName: Brendon Soper Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: All downhill since winning over hurdles at Galway's festival of 2024; well beaten in three of his four runs for this yard and when third in April only five ran; visor added.

RaceName: Brendon Soper Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-14; close third over 2m1f here in June and wasn't beaten that far on his return visit; could be a factor.

RaceName: Brendon Soper Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has a modest strike rate of 1-31 under rules; positive start for this yard when third over hurdles last month but not great since, last week on his return to fences.

RaceName: Brendon Soper Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Behind Man Oh Man here two runs ago but a winner on either side of that, latterly by a length in this new headgear combination; up another 5lb but he's running well.

RaceName: Brendon Soper Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 25th attempt with a comfortable C&D success this month and similar form in defeat last week off this 3lb higher mark; in the mix.

RaceName: Brendon Soper Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Very mixed this year but, with the tongue-tie removed, he went down by a whisker over C&D last month having travelled best; 4lb higher; chance.

RaceName: Brendon Soper Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Still a maiden; wasn't in much form until last week's keeping-on second at Stratford and that effort brings her into it.

RaceName: Brendon Soper Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hard to win with but he's on a competitive mark if new trainer Nick Scholfield can get him back on track; market helpful.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Supporters Of Irish Racing Curragh Cup (Group 2)
Spotlight: Won this event by 5l last year as a prelude to a handsome Irish St Leger success, adding to Group 1 wins in the 2022 National Stakes and in Germany in 2024; finished seventh of 24 in last year's Melbourne Cup; best of three runs this season when third in a Group 1 event over almost 2m at Longchamp; back in his comfort zone trip-wise after Ascot Gold Cup fourth.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Supporters Of Irish Racing Curragh Cup (Group 2)
Spotlight: Strong form at Leopardstown last season, winning three times, including a Listed contest and premier handicap (both over 1m5f on good to yielding); never a factor in the Irish Cesarewitch; unsuited by very soft ground here on seasonal debut; fine effort when runner-up to Scandinavia in the Saval Beg at Leopardstown (1m6f, good).

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Supporters Of Irish Racing Curragh Cup (Group 2)
OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: 2024 Queen's Vase winner; second in last year's Gold Cup at Royal Ascot (good to firm) and runner-up again in the Goodwood Cup (2m, good); flopped when favourite for the Irish St Leger won by Al Riffa; satisfactory return in Ormonde at Chester before a dismal Coronation Cup run; bounced back from that when landing the Queen Alexandra.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Supporters Of Irish Racing Curragh Cup (Group 2)
Spotlight: Group 1-winning 2yo; made the frame last year in the Derby/Irish Derby and career-best when winning the Group 2 Prix Chaudenay (1m7f, very soft) at Longchamp in October; disappointing in a Group 2 Riyadh contest in January; fair third in a 2m Listed race at Down Royal in May.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Supporters Of Irish Racing Curragh Cup (Group 2)
Spotlight: Seven wins on the Flat, also a winning hurdler; landed Gowran conditions race (9.5f, yielding) for a third time last month; has chased home the smart Purview on two occasions at this venue this season; got closer over 1m2f three weeks ago, than in a 1m4f event in May.

RaceName: 1xBet.ie Proud Supporters Of Irish Racing Curragh Cup (Group 2)
Spotlight: Progressive last season, two wins included a Listed contest at Leopardstown (1m6f, good); signed off with creditable fifth in the Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster; bounced back from a poor comeback run at Gowran to land a Group 3 contest over 1m6f at Fairyhouse 11 days ago; stamina assured.

RaceName: PJ McGeever 70th Birthday Celebration Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a point in the spring and is 2-4 since returned to racing under rules for new stable, the latter win gained off today's mark on chasing debut here (2m6f, good to firm) a week ago; likes to make the running, and could be hard to catch on this quick return to hurdling.

RaceName: PJ McGeever 70th Birthday Celebration Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Mrs Janet Cooper & M E Sowersby
Spotlight: Drew clear to clear by 9l over C&D (good) in April, his third hurdling success, and is now only 2lb above that mark but looked out of sorts on his latest outing and is not one to rely upon heavily.

RaceName: PJ McGeever 70th Birthday Celebration Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Safely held when odds-on for stable debut at Warwick (2m5f) in May but, with a tongue-tie fitted, made amends over 3m2f there (also on good) last month; came clear of the others with a progressive rival that day and could still be well handicapped after 5lb rise.

RaceName: PJ McGeever 70th Birthday Celebration Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has mixed record for this stable since last autumn but returned from break to cause 25-1 surprise over fences at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) this month and is now 2-6 for the yard; it's hard to predict whether he'll be in the right mood again here but he has a lower mark over hurdles.

RaceName: Prix Messidor (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: Alain Jathiere, Mme Philippe Demercastel & Nicolas Caullery
Spotlight: Last year's Burj Nahaar winner (1m, dirt) at Meydan who returned to winning ways in a conditions event over 7f at Longchamp in spring; respectable effort in a Group 3 at the same course last month but faces a stiff task at these weights.

RaceName: Prix Messidor (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Steadily progressive sort who ran well in a Group 3 over C&D on fast ground last time; no surprise should he get in the places once again.

RaceName: Prix Messidor (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Group 3 winner at Meydan (1m) and Longchamp (7f) last year but has yet to match either effort since then; shaped with more encouragement back on turf last time, where he's closely matched with Dark Trooper and Fort Payne; others appeal more.

RaceName: Prix Messidor (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Prolific winner but one of his best efforts came in defeat in a strong Listed event at Doha in February; shaped well on his first run since when fourth in Group 3 at Longchamp last time; bit to find with Silius but should be better for that run and likely to get closer back at 1m.

RaceName: Prix Messidor (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Consistent sort who had three of these behind when a short-head second in the 7f Group 3 Porte de la Maillot at Longchamp last time; no problem with the return to this trip and he's likely to give another good account.

RaceName: Prix Messidor (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Turned in her best effort to win soft-ground Listed event over 1m2f on reappearance and a respectable effort over the same trip in Group 3 last time; likely to be found out over this shorter trip on quicker ground, especially if this turns into a dawdle.

RaceName: Prix Messidor (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Progressive colt who made it three wins from as many starts when beating a previous winner in convincing fashion in a Listed event on his AW debut at Deauville last time; yet to race on a sound surface but he's got plenty of fast-ground influences in his pedigree and is an exciting sort who is capable of better; the one to beat.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Held on narrowly at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) last time, opening his account at the fifth attempt; may build on that win and improve further, provided he takes to first-time headgear; shortlisted.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Five-race maiden who has failed to beat a rival in two handicaps this term, running below her 2yo form; chance depends on the effects of first-time headgear.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Inconsistent sort who has been campaigned mostly over 7f, winning once (on AW); only a modest fourth at Redcar most recently; enough to prove back up in distance.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Six-race maiden; has shown bits of promise, including in first-time cheekpieces at Nottingham (upped to 1m2f) most recently; may build on that effort, kept to same trip in the retained headgear.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Has found some consistency (form figures 241) since upped to 1m2f; opened her account in Nottingham event (good to firm) most recently; solid contender off only 2lb higher kept to this distance.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Record is only 1-21 but her record at Redcar includes the sole win and three placed efforts; possibilities off current mark, provided she's in top form and stays this new trip.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Not particularly solid on form but she is the sole handicap newcomer in this field and may be capable of improvement; steps up in distance; market support should be noted.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Has been moving in the right direction switched to turf this term and finished a good second at Leicester (1m2f) most recently; sold out of Hugo Palmer's yard for 12,000gns since; turn may be near.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Six-race maiden who has achieved little; goes beyond 1m for the first time.

RaceName: Zero To Exit Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2m3f/2m5f winner last summer on good ground; he was in lead when he ran out before second-last at Wincanton (3m, good) but followed that a very underwhelming effort; some reservations for now but the ability is there to get involved.

RaceName: Zero To Exit Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Better chaser than hurdler and forgiven latest heavy defeat in a strong handicap; his last hurdle run was four starts back when beaten a neck at Wincanton (2m5f, good); could run well, though some stamina reservations over quite this far.

RaceName: Zero To Exit Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 3m1f winner who has been running well for a while and would have prevailed in another stride over C&D a month ago; up 4lb so has another career-high mark to deal with, but clear claims.

RaceName: Zero To Exit Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Consistent 7yo but not well handicapped and again came up short when third over Fontwell's extended 3m1f when last seen; James Robottom has claimed his 10lb on this gelding's last three starts.

RaceName: Zero To Exit Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Only 3-33 and without a win since 2024; however, arrives here on the back of some solid efforts in defeat, even last time when seventh.

RaceName: Zero To Exit Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Seven-race maiden; 50-1 when tailed off on handicap debut last time over 2m4f.

RaceName: James Nolan Bar Kildare Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: First three runs were at this venue, big improvement on the second occasion to beat the highly-regarded Confucius in a 5f maiden on testing ground; upped to 6f in Group 3 Marble Hill, finished fifth of seven after racing keenly; made a lot of the running before fading into third in a 6f Fairyhouse conditions event; gelded since then.

RaceName: James Nolan Bar Kildare Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Ordinary run on debut at Navan (6f, good); much better when a 2l third at this venue (6f, good to yielding); well held in second at Down Royal (5f, soft); cheekpieces tried now after an inconclusive third placing at Bellewstown.

RaceName: James Nolan Bar Kildare Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Promise on debut at Cork (6f, soft); failed to build on that when soundly beaten at Naas over 5f on quicker ground; fair sixth over C&D last month.

RaceName: James Nolan Bar Kildare Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Similar standard of form in 6f maiden at the Curragh (good) on debut and when fifth in a median sires series race at Fairyhouse (6f, good); needs to find more to get his head in front; headgear.

RaceName: James Nolan Bar Kildare Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Given three runs in nine days in May to get a mark; none of his efforts was particularly notable, but he has been gelded since and brings an intriguing profile to his first handicap, bearing in mind that his trainer has so much trial material in this age group.

RaceName: James Nolan Bar Kildare Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell
Spotlight: Ran to a useful level when second and third in two AW maidens; has finished last in two turf attempts; highly tried in a Listed race last time; not entirely ruled out.

RaceName: James Nolan Bar Kildare Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Did not show much on first two starts; took a step in the right direction when a staying-on sixth of 17 at Navan; may uphold the form of that race with I Ready; interesting.

RaceName: James Nolan Bar Kildare Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Not beaten far when fourth of six on debut at Cork (5f, soft); has struggled twice since then; stable is having a fine season, but juveniles have not contributed significantly so far.

RaceName: Grace And Dotty Conditional Jockeys' Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Runs this track well and posted career-best hurdling performance when scoring over C&D (good to firm) a week ago; 7lb penalty gives her a big weight to carry today but, nevertheless, she has strong claims.

RaceName: Grace And Dotty Conditional Jockeys' Mares' Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Hale Racing, Lyle Farming Company & Ptnr
Spotlight: Useful on the Flat in her prime and came good over hurdles at fifth attempt, battling well for narrow course win (2m, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie) three weeks ago; open to further progress if stamina holds out over this new trip; respected.

RaceName: Grace And Dotty Conditional Jockeys' Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Below form when eighth behind Roarin' Success here last month but won readily in first-time cheekpieces at Worcester (2m, good) nine days ago and now 2-7 over hurdles; moves up in trip; big player if new headgear continues to aid.

RaceName: Grace And Dotty Conditional Jockeys' Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles; two of this year's six runs for Fergal O'Brien offered significant hope but she was soundly beaten on her latest outing; now back in the care of Barry Brennan; others have much less to prove.

RaceName: Grace And Dotty Conditional Jockeys' Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Lightly raced 8yo who struggled when 200-1 for handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in March; big leap of faith required to support her.

RaceName: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap
OwnerName: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor
Spotlight: Is 0-17 but he arrives in good form, placed for third consecutive start when runner-up in 1m2f Ayr handicap (Shifter third) two weeks ago; 1lb worse off with that rival here but he can go very well again.

RaceName: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up at Newcastle on her debut and in decent nick since sent handicapping, most recently prevailing at Nottingham (1m2f, good to firm; well on top at the finish) two days ago when back from a layoff; may build on that win and improve further; respected under 5lb penalty.

RaceName: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Bagged a pair of 8.5f Beverley handicaps this summer but she ran poorly in her hat-trick bid at Pontefract (1m) last time; she's the sort to bounce back though.

RaceName: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap
Spotlight: On a long losing sequence and he was tried without usual cheekpieces when beating just two in 1m2f Leicester handicap 24 days ago; visor is tried again now but others still appeal more.

RaceName: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Arrives in decent nick without winning, fourth of nine in 1m handicap at Ripon 13 days ago; it's now 20 runs since his last victory however.

RaceName: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Two-time course scorer who came in a close third at Ayr (Off Spin in second) before extending her run of good efforts with a close fifth of 12 in 1m2f handicap there 13 days ago; a player once more eased 1lb.

RaceName: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who gained a first win for Daragh Bourke in classified event at Wolverhampton (8.5f) last March; lightly raced since and only ninth of 14 in 1m Newcastle handicap in April; since had a wind op (tongue-strap also goes back on) so can't be discounted after a break.

RaceName: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Is 0-17 and he failed to build on earlier promise with sixth of eight in 7f classified event at Ayr last month; others are preferred.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Pulled up in a hunter chase in 2025 on previous run over regulation fences; this multiple point winner can be given another chance and he's performed okay in two maiden hurdles for this yard.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Lightly raced and he's 2-2 over fences, at Southwell 40 days ago (about 3m1f) defying a 13lb rise and a 393-day absence with a narrow verdict; if he can back that up then a 3lb nudge shouldn't be beyond him.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Four-time chase winner but badly out of sorts before finishing second to a surprise winner over C&D 16 days ago (visor returned); remains to be seen if he'll be as good again and he may need more anyway.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: C&D winner last summer at the expense of Testflight who beat her next time in this race; another win on fast ground at Stratford soon after before a 20l defeat here; high in the weights now and she's been absent 11 months.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: The last of four chase wins was in August 2024; she's building some momentum after a long absence having pushed Edgewell close over C&D a month ago; that was under a 10lb claimer but she has to be considered.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 1-12 over fences; below par for a while now, including on recent stable debut; new headgear combination.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Sole career success came in this race 12 months ago and a recent close fourth here behind Edgewell should have teed him up for his defence; nothing wrong with his mark for all that he's 1lb wrong.

RaceName: Final Furlong Podcast With 1xBet.ie Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Three-time Grade 1-winning chaser makes his Flat debut here at the age of eight; his dam is an unraced half-sister to a German 1m Listed winner; it's hard to know what to expect from him, but he's an intriguing runner and hails from a savvy yard in great form.

RaceName: Final Furlong Podcast With 1xBet.ie Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Frankel gelding ran poorly on debut at Dundalk when missing the break (1m); much better at Cork (10.5f, soft) on seasonal return when showing signs of greenness but just denied by a head; gelded since then and heads the list of likely candidates.

RaceName: Final Furlong Podcast With 1xBet.ie Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Both runs to date came over 1m2f here; was an eyecatcher on debut (good) when keeping on for a 1.75l fourth; nothing went right for him last time when slowly away, racing wide and hitting the front too early, but was still a creditable second; step up in trip should suit and he's very interesting with some better luck.

RaceName: Final Furlong Podcast With 1xBet.ie Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Sets the standard on 83 after seven runs; one of his best efforts came in a Down Royal handicap last time (1m5f, soft) when headed late on into second; a repeat of that could see him go close.

RaceName: Final Furlong Podcast With 1xBet.ie Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Distant fourth behind a runaway winner on debut at Dundalk in May (winner subsequently placed in a Royal Ascot handicap); was 1.25l behind the third horse, who won easily next time; could outrun his likely odds.

RaceName: Final Furlong Podcast With 1xBet.ie Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Creditable first run over 1m2f here last month when outpaced late on but taking sixth of 11 (good to firm, 100-30); not unfancied that day; needs a bit more.

RaceName: Final Furlong Podcast With 1xBet.ie Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Took a huge step forward from her 1m2f debut here when upped to 1m5f at Leopardstown (good to firm) in a newly-added tongue tie, headed into second in the dying strides; benefitted from being ridden positively that day and should be thereabouts.
